# Maintenance Lane

Use for small bug fixes, docs cleanup, stale-file checks, fixture repair, status reports, low-token work, and narrow harness upkeep.

Pass example: "Fix one broken fixture assertion and rerun the focused test."

Fail example: "Do a general polish pass that turns into a full product redesign."

More pass examples:

- "Correct a typo in a Golden Gameplan checkpoint and rerun the docs-focused test."
- "Patch a fixture path mismatch in one test helper and record the before/after command."
- "Add a missing audit note without changing runtime behavior."
- "Update a stale status report when no product code is touched."

More fail examples:

- "While fixing a typo, redesign the dashboard layout and change the product promise."
- "Clean up docs by rewriting all customer-facing copy across the app."
- "Update dependencies as part of routine maintenance without explicit package approval."
- "Keep iterating on visual polish after the done contract has moved to taste gate."

Required evidence: issue fixed, files changed, focused test/check command, before/after note, reason the change stayed small.

Forbidden by default: broad copy rewrites, new product features, large visual redesigns, dependency updates without approval, vague polish.
